review_text: I love the story telling of it

If you took a mans life, and told any single director to make it as interesting as this movie was, I would bet you that never would somebody have done anything even CLOSE to what Christopher Nolan did with this movie. The Visuals, and the audio was so immersive, and the raw passion you saw from the actors was even more impressive. This movie is a hand picked masterpiece from one of the worlds most acclaimed directors. To the people who missed out watching this is theatres. You missed out on the best movie of the year in which it came out. Although the length of the movie is a big talk, you're paying for a movie, you better get a damn long movie. You can't summarize a revolutionary breakthrough in 1 hour. To those people who argue this, I'd say go watch a romance.

Incredibly dull and pretentious film. No matter how hard they try to insist this is great, it is unwatchable. I have a high tolerance for dialogue and patient filmmaking, I wasn't expecting an action film. But there just isn't a reason to care about what they're droning on about. The visual gimmicks can't come close to making this entertaining, I can't believe all the positive reviews.
It's not a Friday night movie.
It’s an amazing movie with real physics equations. However, it’s not 100% real, drama changed the course of the actions and it’s not based on the real events.

Masterpice of biopics WOW. I don't really understand how somebody can create such a great movie with almost no action at all. No, like really, in this movie,e 90% is talking, and 10% is action of A-Bomb. Nolan really did a biopic with the tension of an action thriller. I will not talk much about the performances of the cast, but I must say that RDJ stole the acting aspect of this movie for me. He is Iron Man/Tony Stark, but he is much, much more. To finish this up, I must say that I actually think this movie would be an 8, if not a phenomenal soundtrack that acts like a glue that makes this movie a masterpiece.
Excellent script and cinematography
Very long and very boring. The acting was good but not enough to make me watch it again.
i am so in love with this movie as a STEM student its amazing to see a film portray science in a way that it can be fascinating but destructive

great movie, nice to see Robert Downey Jr in it and Cillian Murphy is great as Oppenheimer, great biography movie about Oppenheimer and his creation of the Nuclear Bomb and the music is incredible. the only thing stopping it being a 5 is it is way too long and is the type of movie you only watch once. if it had more rewatchabillity and maybe 2 hours and 30 minutes it would have been a 5

Hands down Christopher Nolan's best film! And that's saying something considering his stacked filmography. This film is a masterpiece in every single category. Directing, acting, cinematography, score, and much more. In a way, this film is a tragedy, the tragedy of Oppenheimer. The way it makes you feel when Truman is on the radio and everyone is cheering is absolutely haunting, as is almost everything after the trinity test. The ending is one of, if not, the best ending I've ever seen! Truly a masterpiece of modern cinema!

Brilliant, masterful, and a movie that will remain relevant for years to come. It shows how the atomic bomb ended World War II but also marked the true beginning of the Cold War, which has shaped global politics ever since. It is primarily a character study disguised as a historical epic. The nonlinear editing mirrors memory, testimony, and accusation, which are central themes. The story is less about "what happened" and more about how history judges people. Anyone complaining about the constant flash-forward and flashback structure do not understand how essential this storytelling approach is for the film to unfold properly and deliver its final impact. Nolan is especially skilled with this narrative style, as seen in his film Memento, where the technique truly shines. He is definitely one of the best directors of our time, alongside Denis Villeneuve, the Coen Brothers, Martin Scorsese, and Quentin Tarantino.
It's shot like a string of short jumpy vids mashed together.

Another Masterpiece. Christopher Nolan managed to give us a fascinating biopic on J. Robert Oppenheimer the creator of the atomic bomb. I think this film was amazing the explosions and cinematography were cool. Cilian Murphy’s performance was amazing. This was my first time seeing him act and he really impressed me. My problems with the movie are is that there is no resolution. When Oppenheimer and Lewis Straus were fighting I thought there was going to be A Solution. Also I didn’t like how the movie ends with Einstein leaving and Oppenheimer opens his eyes and see the world exploding that’s the part that got me saying “What”. Also RDJ’s Performance was perfect. This Just Shows that RDJ can still act without being in a suit of armor. Also who would’ve thought that Josh from DRAKE AND JOSH would be pushing the detonator. Anyways I still think Interstellar is still better but this Film is easily a Top 3 Nolan Film. Overall I’m Rating This Film A 9.5/10

This may be a great film but the execrable sound quality, intrusive score, and mumbling during crucial scenes rendered it frustratingly incoherent. There were very important scenes that were scarcely audible and this dissipated the tension and was SO disappointing.

I’ve watched a lot of movies in my life up to seeing this but this movie made me realize how much I adore film as not only cinema but as art. This film has incredible writing and directing from Nolan. An Incredible and stellar cast with stand out performances from cillian murphy, Robert Downey Jr., and Rami Malek. This movie is my all time favorite, it helped me open my eyes to what film really is and how directors, writers, and actors can bring such a masterpiece to screen.

Visually astounding, well-structured dialogues, and a storytelling style that only Christopher Nola could deliver perfectly. Absolutely recommend, every second of the movie kept me engaged from the very first second to the very last. If I could say anything wrong about this movie, I’d say that it doesn’t last long enough. Historical accuracy might be biased towards American side, though its bittersweet plot hinders it perfectly as if it seemed to be an impartially-based documentary/biopic. Casting fits perfectly to the narrative, Cillian Murphy’s acting portrays utterly Dr. J. Robert Oppenheimer alikeness physically and personally, almost bringing him back from death. Matt Damon, Emely Blunt and Rami Malek were also phenomenally great as they do. As a first timer watching this movie, seeing Robert Downey Jr. as villain-like character shocked me a bit; needless to say he has performed in many other movies as such, but this time he definitely did amazing.
Simply amazing film.

There will never be another film that deals with the story of the creation of atomic weapons that literally changed the world.........however this is not a rewatch able movie but it is an important movie.

Que filme MARAVILHOSO. Eu queria obrigadar cada ser humano deste planeta a assistir só para cada um ter a real noção do impacto das bombas nucleares. Pra assistir esse filme é preciso muita atenção, porque qualquer distração já atrapalha toda a experiência. A direção do Chirstopher Nola tá maravilhosa e a atuação Cillian Murphy está PERFEITA, PERFEITA. O que esse homem fez aqui é coisa de outro mundo. Todos os oscars que esse filme aqui levou são muito mais que merecidos. Tem cenas MUITO, mas MUITO BEM FEITAS (aquela em que ele discursa para o público depois do lançamento da bomba é TÃO PERFEITA! A melhor do filme). E a atuação, como eu disse, é maravilhosa. Você consegue saber tudo que os personagens estão pensando apenas com o OLHAR. São três horas de filme muito bem feitas e aproveitadas). Enfim, filme PERFEITO e muito necessário.

Christopher Nolan once again delivers. I loved this film. The reasoning behind shooting some of the scenes in black and white to show RDJs character’s perspective was brilliant. Christopher Nolan is a genius. The best in the game right now. Not to forget the lead and supporting characters performances which blew everything else from this year out of the park.

Los efectos especiales y la banda sonora 10/10. Te amo Albert Einstein xd
This movie teaches me and inspires me to grow up, iisten more and be a radical thinker and critical thinker

Late to the party but man this is good. This is a movie that is mostly people talking for 3 hours but it's able to be so compelling, engaging & you can't take your eyes off the screen thats when you know you have made something truly special. This is movie that I need to watch again at some piont as I feel this is the kind of film that would somehow even better on a rewatch. But the acting, music, cinematography, sound effects and the story itself was just truly excellent. It also makes you think about some of the moral implications of this event and the fact that humans can make something so dangerous & destructive. Just struggled through 'Oppenheimer' on Netflix. Advice to Christopher Nolan: When telling a subtle, multi-story, oodles of key characters tale of 30 years in a very interesting man's life, avoid doing it in constant flash-forward, flash-back style. From about 30 minutes in, I was constantly pausing to check Wikipedia, clueless to "what's going on here" and "who are these people." Must've been a nightmare watch in a theater. All that said (and as so often is the case in prestige productions), great acting, Cillian Murphy and Florence Pugh especially excellent. And Nolan executes/designs almost every individual scene very well. So, could've been a near great movie, though focusing so much on the Strauss vendetta side story was also a deep flaw. US cold war 'commie under every bed' shenanigans is both very 'been done' and small potatoes compared to the main 'destroyer of worlds' story, which seemed to be glided over at times.

Film was very drawn out, with several sections where nothing much really happened. The soundtrack was for the most part excellent, but sometimes felt like it was making scenes more interesting than they actually were, and in some instances drowning out dialogue. The least impressive part was the explosion, which totally took me out of the story. I can understand a directors desire to keep the explosion practical, but when it falls this flat it should have been re-done. No typical Hollywood petrol explosion can get across the significance and energy of the Trinity test, despite some clever camera work.
If you know you won’t like this movie, do NOT let your guy friend convince you to watch it. You’ll be lost halfway through wishing you could sleep without the disturbance of atomic bomb noises.

The movie insists upon itself. It seems that there has been much more effort put into convincing the viewer, that this is a great and dramatic film with a lot of tension, rather than the movie being those things. A simple example that describes this major issue which prevents me from enjoying the film, is how it uses dramatic music not to underline dramatic scenes, but to make bland scenes seem dramatic
Almost perfect. Just a few moments that seemed on the nose but still loved it.

The visuals and sound design are incredible and the obvious standout in this movie. However this movie is way too long and could probably cut close to half the runtime. There is way to many unnecessary scenes and dialog. If I could go back in time i would have walked out of the theater right after that bomb goes off the rest of the movie is 45 minutes of jury duty.
Not my favorite Nolan film, but definitely one of his best. The performances alone make it a great film, nevermind the score, effects, & writing.
Too much unnecessary dialogue drowned out the movie!

I don't get the hype behind nolan, i know he is a good director and I love some of his work , especially the Batman films but this was for me awful, I know most people disagree with me on this but I want films to be entertaining and not ones to be boring or mind f**ka like these , I really wanted to like this but sorry I couldn't, 2/10

Satisfying as a historical thriller than a biopic. We learn little about the real-life figure beyond the obvious. Kudos to Jennifer Lame for her terrific editing; she matches the relentless momentum of Nolan’s screenplay and weaves the four timelines together without ever becoming confusing or letting our attention waver.

Nolan does it again, personally thought the dark knight was him at his best but Oppenheimer exceeded all expectations. The running time is justified, the cast was accurate and the story well constructed. Intense, dark, sinister, catastrophic, words you could use to describe this movie but the list goes on. Anyone finding a reason to downgrade the movie is actually admitting to outstanding it really was. Next stop… The Odyssey!
Just saw it for the first time. Boring. Too many things that are so irritating due to level very close to floor. Didn't like it really.

Oppenheimer blew me away — it’s a masterpiece of cinema. From the breathtaking visuals and haunting score to the intense performances and heavy-hitting story, this film grabs you by the heart and mind and doesn’t let go. It’s more than just a movie — it’s an unforgettable experience that makes you think, feel, and question. If you want a film that truly shakes you, challenges you, and stays with you long after it ends, Oppenheimer is 5 out of 5, no doubt.
Absolute Cinema!! There is some boring scene, but the cinematography is amazing! Nolan did a good job to this film.

I have a question: What is this brilliance, Nolan? I wasn't bored at all. The cinematography was superb, the directing legendary, and the team's creativity was astonishing, especially the brilliant actor Cillian Murphy. I want to thank everyone who contributed to the production of this masterpiece. It definitely deserves an Oscar. Thank you for the most wonderful three hours. I didn't expect it to be this amazing, a true work of art. This film has reached perfect perfection.

all over the place, random sex scenes that didnt hold much weight besides the insanity of oppenheimer, the story of the most powerful bomb never has a detination stronger than that of a gasoline bomb. No I didnt want them to use an atomic bomb but grab some cgi to make it sligthly more favorable, yes there is a battle between morality and clarity, so that was good I guess, Nolan is by far my favorite director, but honwstly this really could have been made into a documentary and been 80% as good. There will be a lot of Nolan fan boys that like this just becasue they like all his other stuff, but this one bombed for sure.

An absolute gem of a movie by Christopher Nolan. Not a single dull moment in the entire movie. The protagonist acted fabulously to portray the complex character of Oppenheimer. Loved the ending of the movie which provokes deep introspection -- especially the scene between Einstein and Oppenheimer. Einstein quietly warns Oppenheimer that once the world has inflicted sufficient punishment, it will shower him with medals and proclaim forgiveness—not for Oppenheimer’s sake, but to soothe its own guilty conscience. This exchange lays bare how Oppenheimer’s towering achievement has morphed into a lifelong burden of regret, and the eventual honors will serve less as genuine recognition than as a public ritual of absolution for everyone else. The story is also a sobering reminder that the intellectuals who create unimaginable powerful weapons or systems do not actually play a role in deciding how it should be used. The moment crystallizes the crushing moral and existential load Oppenheimer now bears, having grasped too late the devastating fallout of what he unleashed. In the end, their brief conversation stands as a somber meditation on the scientist’s responsibility and the terrifying, often catastrophic unintended consequences of technological advancement. Today we stand an another threshold -- of AI disrupting our lives both in positive and negative ways. Only time will tell the unintended consequences of the AI Revolution that we are experiencing today!

With excellent performances, excellent cinematography, excellent score, and excellent effects, Oppenheimer is Nolan at the top of his technical game. Balancing out the spectacle, the film brings equally hard hitting character moments and ethical quandaries which will stick with you long after the credits roll. Although perhaps slightly longer than necessary, with occasional moments of drag, it hardly registers next to the incredible talent on display in every facet of this film.

I was confused with this movie, which would make sense because I’m an adolescent and I’m not into a lot of science, but Cillian Murphy‘s performance was epic, Iron Man himself was great, the music was top notch, and THE ENDING was a masterpiece. I’ll recommend this movie to people who like war movies.
This is a biopic for the thinking person, for the viewer who is interested in history, politics, and most of all, ethics. Rendered beautifully and effectively, the performances are all top-notch.
Oppenheimer is the perfect movie ♥️

It was okay. Kind of a little all over the place. My favorite parts were all of Cicillian Murphy's scenes as Robert J. Oppenheimer. He really steals the show. However, there were SOOOO many scenes with characters that talk FOREVER and it kind of bored me like crazy in some places. I'm probably biased because I'm not a history buff but if you are I'm sure you'll enjoy it.

Very good acting, the story is fantastic. Some parts of the movie can be confusing if you don’t know the history that well. I often times had to rewind just to understand the context. Overall a good movie!
I made it almost 2 hours into film and could stomach another hour of this slog of a film. Beautiful cinematography, brilliant music and decent acting could not save a dull uninteresting plot.
bureaucrats and courts arguing about whether we should create the destroyer of worlds for 3 hours? sleep inducing boredom pit. most of it's made up anyway, go paint the front gate instead.
